title: Bay-leaf burn: the number that scares you, released into smoke
subtitle: A candle ritual for writing a financial fear on a bay leaf and releasing it.
excerpt: A short folk-magic ritual for releasing a specific number or financial fear by writing it on a bay leaf and burning it. Use on Day 16 of the MONEY program or any time a specific money figure is carrying too much weight.
sourceNotes: Bay leaf burning is a widely practised candle and fire ritual drawn from folk magic traditions across multiple cultures, particularly the Mediterranean, where bay laurel has long associations with purification and release. Origin to homemade.education as a companion to Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when a specific number — a debt figure, a target income, a price you are afraid to charge — is sitting in your chest with too much weight.
practice.whenNotToUse: Do not do this anywhere near curtains, fabric, or anything flammable. Use a ceramic bowl or a sink. Never leave lit materials unattended.

content.text: A folk-magic fire ritual for Day 16 of the MONEY program: releasing the number that scares you by committing it to smoke. Short, simple, uses dried bay leaves and a candle or matches.
One or two dried bay leaves (from the supermarket spice aisle)
A ceramic bowl or a sink, for safe burning

Take the bay leaf and the pen. Write on the leaf the number, figure, or financial fear that is currently carrying too much weight. It might be a debt total. A price you're afraid to charge. An income figure that feels impossible. Write it clearly.
Hold the leaf in both hands for a moment. Say, out loud or in your mind: "I am ready to release the fear around this number. I release it now."
Light the leaf at one end, using the candle or a match. Hold it over the ceramic bowl or the sink. Let it burn.
Watch the smoke rise. The number is leaving. It was never the number that had the power — it was the fear around it.
Once the leaf has burned, run water over any remaining ash if using a sink, or leave ash in the bowl to cool completely before disposing of it.

Bay leaf burning is a widely practised ritual in folk magic traditions across many cultures, particularly those of the Mediterranean, where bay laurel has long been associated with purification and the release of what is no longer needed. Adapted here as a companion ritual for Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
